steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Recall the area of a square
steps.content: For a square with side length $s$, the area is $A = s^2 = s \cdot s$.

steps.title: Substitute the given side length
steps.content: The panel's side length is $7.25w$ meters, so $A = (7.25w)(7.25w)$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A ($A=(w+7.25)(w+7.25)$): adds the numbers instead of multiplying; the side is $7.25w$, not $w+7.25$.
  • Choice B ($A=(4)(7.25w)$): that is a perimeter expression ($4s$), not an area.
  • Choice C ($A=(7.25w)(7.25w)$): correctly squares the side length $7.25w$.
  • Choice D ($A=(7.25/w)(7.25/w)$): divides instead of multiplies, so the units and value are wrong.
That confirms C is correct.
